_comment: ubuntnix archive lockfile (SPEC.md §4.4; GitHub issue #8, milestone M1). Generated by bin/ubx-resolve: apt-solver-based resolution of the declared package set in archive.packages.json, run against the real apt solver against an isolated rootdir pointed at this snapshot.ubuntu.com timestamp -- see bin/ubx-resolve's header for the full mechanism. Two-tier per the reproducibility model: `public` pins the snapshot timestamp plus resolved (name, version, arch, component, path, sha256, size) tuples for the archive.ubuntu.com/security.ubuntu.com pockets the snapshot service covers; `esm` uses the identical per-package schema but is intentionally an empty list until milestone M4 wires up esm.ubuntu.com fetching against a Pro token (no snapshot service exists for esm -- SPEC.md §4.4, §8.2, R4). Consumed by nix/archive.nix via builtins.fromJSON; see that file for fetch mechanics and tests/lib/validate-archive-lockfile.py for the schema this file is held to. Every sha256 below was recomputed by bin/ubx-resolve from the actually-downloaded .deb bytes -- apt's own index-reported checksum is never trusted blindly. Packages are sorted by name for a stable diff and a byte-identical re-resolution given unchanged inputs.

esm._comment: Empty until M4 (SPEC.md §4.4, R4): esm.ubuntu.com has no snapshot service, so this tier is pinned directly by (name, version, arch, component, path, sha256, size) once M4 wires up fetching against a Pro token. esm content is subscription-gated and never redistributed publicly.
